Annual Report Data Analysis - Question 2.01 and 2.02

Cristin Young and Tristan Burgess

2026-04-07

2. Did the EBP/CDEP Workstream increase access to services for targeted populations?

Question 2.01: Did successful transition rates out of EBP/CDEP services differ by location?

THREE inclusion criteria: 1. must have initial time point data (i.e., be enrolled in an intervention), 2. must have a valid reason for discharge selected at time of discharge (i.e., excludes those with “no reason selected”), 3. client must be attached to a specific grantee location.

% successful completion at discharge by location

% successful completion at discharge by region

Mapping by region

Mapping by primary county

  geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(name)), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Region") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "California Census Regions (1–10)")
Error in geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(name)), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +  : 
  non-numeric argument to binary operator

% of those who met treatment goals (successful completion + improved score) by location

Question 2.02: Did successful transition rates out of EBP/CDEP services differ by subpopulations?

Race

Race summary

Roll detailed race variables up based on first answer into: Asian, Black or African American, Don’t Know, Hispanic or Latino, Middle Eastern or North African, Native American/American Indian or Alaska Native, Native Hawaiian or Pacific Islander, Not Applicable, Prefer Not to Answer, White.

Ethnicity

Codebook: Chinese = 1, Asian Indian = 2, Filipino = 3, Vietnamese = 4, Korean = 5, Japanese = 6, African American = 7, Jamaican = 8, Haitian = 9, Nigerian = 10, Somali = 11, Mexican = 12, Puerto Rican = 13, Salvadoran = 14, Cuban = 15, Dominican = 16, Guatemalan = 17, Native Hawaiian = 18, Samoan = 19, Chamorro = 20, English = 21, German = 22, Irish = 23, Italian = 24, Polish = 25, Scottish = 26, 2+ ethnicities selected = 88

Gender identity

Sexual orientation

Insurance status
